page: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. It features 160 vCPUs mapped to 80 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 3844 GB of dedicated RAM, yielding approximately 24.02 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The server exhibits poor single-core performance, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng and Geekbench tests. However, its multi-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached and uncached large block transfers ranking in the top 25%. Conversely, the Passmark memory score is weak, falling into the bottom 25%.

Given its massive memory footprint and strong multi-core throughput, this instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and heavy data processing. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput both rank in the top 25%. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of memory relative to vCPUs makes it a specialized choice where memory capacity, rather than raw single-threaded CPU speed, is the primary constraint.
description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized server instance configured with 160 vCPUs and 3844 GB of dedicated RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 80 physical cores running at 2.2 GHz, this instance is designed for memory-intensive applications. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks show a significant contrast: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are strong, placing in the top 25%. This profile makes the instance highly effective for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, and high-throughput static web serving, where massive memory capacity and parallel processing capabilities outweigh single-threaded execution speeds.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 is a memory-optimized instance with 160 vCPUs and 3844 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m1-ultramem-160 offers 160 vCPUs and 3844 GB RAM, optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring massive RAM capacity and strong multi-core throughput for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 160 vCPUs and 3844 GB of dedicated RAM
Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture with 80 physical cores
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and Redis deployments
